notes: Author quests in CK, then inspect stages, aliases, scripts, and accidental edits in xEdit.
Use CK for dialogue authoring and conditions; use xEdit to audit records and conflicts after saving.
CK owns alias setup; verify persistence assumptions and VMAD/property state before release.

task: Create or edit magic effects and ability spells
notes: Use CK for archetypes, conditions, UI flags, and spell/effect relationships; use extracted palettes for discovery only.
Use xEdit to see winners, losers, and patch candidates; test runtime when conflict behavior matters.

task: Forward conflicts into a compatibility patch
notes: Patch by intended fields, not by color alone; prefer separate patch plugins over editing original mods.

task: Clean dirty overrides and unwanted masters
notes: Manual review comes first; QuickAutoClean is useful for release plugins but does not replace author judgment.

task: Review plugin masters and file header
notes: Verify plugin type, masters, and header behavior in xEdit; version-sensitive behavior should be checked against xEdit release notes.

task: Plan ESL or ESPFE release shape
notes: Confirm record count, compacting needs, header behavior, and downstream patch impact before changing plugin type.
Use EditorID plus source plugin in docs; do not treat runtime load-order prefixes as stable.

task: Create placed references, cells, or navmesh
notes: Use CK for spatial authoring; inspect broad overrides and NAVI/navmesh side effects after saving.

task: Curate leveled lists or distribution edits
notes: Use extraction for discovery and xEdit for patch review; test distribution in representative load orders.
Include parseable version info and specific load-order rationale when relevant; avoid vague load-last guidance.
Add tags only when the Bashed Patch should preserve the plugin's change; do not tag unimportant edits.
Run CK in the active mod-manager profile and verify scripts and plugin visibility before authoring.
Verify CKPE supports the installed CK version and review CKPE settings before treating warnings or Unicode behavior as solved.

task: Audit Papyrus VMAD after script changes
notes: Use CK for property wiring and xEdit for VMAD inspection; test old-save migration when properties change.
Use current CK asset tooling where applicable; verify paths, archives, facegen, scripts, and loose-file assumptions in game.
